action.reasoning: Observation: full-position pairwise ridge remains best on hidden test (0.5969), while the 28-position window model is weaker (0.4836) but may contain complementary localized signal. Analysis: with limited time, hidden-eval-guided lightweight ensembling is the fastest path; need first add a pickle-safe ensemble class in model.py and produce an ensemble checkpoint. Decision: create WeightedEnsembleModel, save an 80/20 baseline/window blend, smoke-test predict.py, and submit it for evaluation.
action.command: python make_ensemble.py --out checkpoints/ens_b80_w20.pkl --w_base 0.8 --w_window 0.2 && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/ens_b80_w20.pkl --output predictions_ens_smoke.json
